hôpital.text: The circumflex marks a lost s — old 'hospital', kin to English.

château.text: Circumflex = a dropped s (castel); -eau ending — a castle.

oiseau.text: Holds all five vowels, and not one is pronounced as written — a bird.

accueil.text: The u after c keeps the hard sound before e — a welcome.

orgueil.text: Same 'ueil' spelling that keeps c/g hard — pride.

tournesol.text: tourne (turns) + sol (sun) — a sunflower that follows the light.

rythme.text: From Greek; a silent-final e and a y — rhythm.

quincaillerie.text: A hardware shop; qu, ill and -erie stacked — a spelling marathon.
